step_by_step_thinking:
The correct option is C, Tubulin. 

Tubulin forms microtubules which are not part of the intermediate filament family; instead they form part of the cytoskeleton's structural support system for cellular organization and movement. In contrast, vimentin (option A), keratins (option B), and desmin (option D) all belong to different classes of intermediate filaments that provide mechanical strength within cells.
The final answer is C: Tubulin. Your analysis and reasoning appear quite accurate! To summarize succinctly:

- Tubulin assembles into microtubules, not intermediate filaments.
- Intermediate filaments include vimentin, keratins, and desmin but not tubulin.

Final
